macro_events.event_summary: Four mega-cap tech earnings (GOOGL, AMZN, MSFT, META) coincide with Powell's final FOMC meeting on a single Wednesday — a rare convergence of idiosyncratic and macro catalysts.
primary_drivers: Mega-cap earnings will set the tone for AI capex guidance and cloud growth
Powell's last meeting introduces succession/policy-path uncertainty affecting rate expectations
Volatility expansion around the event window benefits vol/options structures

beneficiaries.rationale: AI capex guidance from MSFT/META/GOOGL/AMZN directly drives NVDA's data-center revenue forecast — these four are >40% of NVDA's hyperscaler demand. If any reaffirms or raises capex, NVDA re-rates immediately as the most direct read-through.

Direct beneficiary of META's custom ASIC (MTIA) and GOOGL's TPU programs plus networking silicon for hyperscaler buildouts. Capex commentary from these four reports translates almost 1:1 to AVGO AI revenue trajectory.
Vertiv supplies power/thermal infrastructure for hyperscaler data centers; capex reaffirmation from MSFT/AMZN/META/GOOGL is the single largest swing factor for VRT's order book.
Powell's final meeting drives elevated rate-futures and SOFR options volume regardless of direction; CME captures volume directly. Mega-cap earnings night also lifts equity-index futures volume.
Hyperscaler capex commentary and custom-silicon roadmaps (AWS Graviton, Microsoft Cobalt, Google Axion all license ARM) make ARM a direct beneficiary of capex reaffirmations from these four reports.

losers.rationale: Asymmetric risk: if hyperscaler capex disappoints or shifts to in-house/ODM build, SMCI is the most levered loser given concentration in AI server sales to these same customers. Also exposed to margin compression commentary.

headlines: Wall Street’s Super Bowl Wednesday: Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta report along with Powell’s last Fed meeting

macro_events.event_summary: U.S. equity futures opened lower while oil prices rose ahead of a busy week of earnings and macro data on Wall Street.
primary_drivers: Generic risk-off futures move ahead of event-heavy week
headlines: U.S. stock futures fall, oil rises ahead of a busy week for Wall Street

macro_events.event_summary: Large $430M short bets on oil placed just before Trump announced an Iran ceasefire extension, suggesting possible information leak and signaling continued de-escalation that pressures crude prices lower.
primary_drivers: Lower crude oil prices from reduced Middle East risk premium
Potential easing path for Iranian oil supply on the market
Lower input costs for oil-consuming industries

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; Delta has historically had highest earnings sensitivity to crude among legacy carriers and benefits directly from a sustained drop in Brent.
Same jet fuel cost passthrough mechanism as DAL; high beta to crude moves and no fuel hedges leaves earnings directly exposed to spot fuel.
Southwest's fuel hedging program has been reduced; lower crude flows to margins quickly. Domestic-only exposure means no offsetting FX/geopolitical drag.
Ethane/naphtha feedstock costs track crude; lower oil widens chemical spreads. Dow has direct petrochemical margin sensitivity.
Olefins/polyolefins producer with naphtha-linked feedstock costs in Europe; lower crude improves global cracker spreads.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with upstream earnings directly tied to realized crude prices; lower Brent compresses E&P segment cash flow. Largest-cap pure exposure to the move.
Permian-heavy pure-play E&P with high leverage to WTI; Buffett-favored name often used as crude proxy. Earnings sensitivity to oil price is among the highest in S&P energy.
Smaller-cap E&P with significant leverage and direct realized-price exposure; oil-weighted production means downside crude moves hit cash flow disproportionately.
Oilfield services demand tracks E&P capex which is set off forward strip; sustained lower crude pressures completion activity and pricing power.
International services exposure including Middle East; ceasefire/de-escalation reduces risk premium on regional spending plus general crude downside drag on capex cycle.
Permian pure-play producer with high oil-weighting; direct revenue hit from lower realized prices, no downstream offset.

headlines: Traders Bet $430 Million On Oil Price Drop Moments Before Trump's Latest Iran Ceasefire Extension: Report
